key_insight: A coupled CEL simulation and ANN models can accurately predict weld temperature, axial force, and defect formation in HDPE friction stir welding, enabling fast identification of robust process windows.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that bottom-surface preheating and moderate rotation/travel settings can reduce force fluctuations, improve thermal uniformity, and prevent wormhole defects in thermoplastic joining.
method: 3D coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) model for FSW thermo-mechanics and material flow
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental data for temporal temperature and axial-force prediction
Process-map generation across rotational speed, welding speed, and preheating conditions
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved high predictive accuracy with R2 values between 0.91 and 0.99, and the CEL results matched experiments for void formation, thermal response, and mechanical loading.
watch_item: The ANN and process maps are tied to the tested HDPE geometry and parameter range, so extrapolation to other tools, materials, or thicknesses should be validated experimentally.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
